channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Claude is Offline: Anthropic's AI Chatbot Hits a Global Wall

media:content.media:description: Outage reports reveal that roughly 62% of users are completely locked out

item.description: Hundreds of users are reporting major outages as Anthropic says a fix is already in the works.
item.content:encoded: Hundreds of users are reporting major outages as Anthropic says a fix is already in the works.

item.title: "Dig Of Century": Ancient Coins, Medieval Pottery Unearthed From Notre Dame

media:content.media:description: A slice of Notre Dame's forecourt has become an excavation site.

item.description: At the cathedral's birth in 1163, the entire square was packed with medieval houses, split by a single street.
item.content:encoded: At the cathedral's birth in 1163, the entire square was packed with medieval houses, split by a single street.

item.title: 'Brought Shame On Religion': Sikh Man Jailed For Stabbing UK Teen Over Racism

media:content.media:description: Vickrum Digwa lied to the police about being the victim of a racist attack

item.description: Judge William Mousley of the Southampton Crown Court said that Digwa killed Nowak with a large knife that he kept attached to his belt. Mousley said that Nowak asked the Digwa if he was a "bad man", which led him to feel that he was insulted.
item.content:encoded: Judge William Mousley of the Southampton Crown Court said that Digwa killed Nowak with a large knife that he kept attached to his belt. Mousley said that Nowak asked the Digwa if he was a "bad man", which led him to feel that he was insulted.

item.title: UK Now Using Elon Musk's Militarised Satellite Network Starshield: Report

media:content.media:description: The British military began using Starlink in July 2022.

item.description: Britain's defence ministry started transitioning operational military traffic to the more expensive Starshield service around the beginning of this year.
item.content:encoded: Britain's defence ministry started transitioning operational military traffic to the more expensive Starshield service around the beginning of this year.

item.title: Hackers Trick Meta's AI To Infiltrate Instagram Accounts, Including Obama-Era White House Archive

media:content.media:description: Meta spokesperson Andy Stone confirmed that the vulnerability has since been fixed.

item.description: Among the accounts reportedly compromised were the Obama-era White House archive account, beauty retailer Sephora, US Space Force Chief Master Sergeant John Bentivegna, and cybersecurity researcher Jane Manchun Wong.
item.content:encoded: Among the accounts reportedly compromised were the Obama-era White House archive account, beauty retailer Sephora, US Space Force Chief Master Sergeant John Bentivegna, and cybersecurity researcher Jane Manchun Wong.

item.title: The $15 Billion 'Floating City': Inside The Vision For The World's Largest Ship. See Pics

media:content.media:description: The vessel would continuously travel around the globe.

item.description: If built, the vessel would be unlike anything ever seen in maritime history, carrying up to 80,000 people and dwarfing even today's largest cruise ships.
item.content:encoded: If built, the vessel would be unlike anything ever seen in maritime history, carrying up to 80,000 people and dwarfing even today's largest cruise ships.

item.title: Climate Change Will Make Super El Nino Especially Severe, Scientists Warn

media:content.media:description: WMO forecasts suggest a strong El Nino is possible.

item.description: The World Meteorological Organisation said there is an 80 per cent chance that an El Nino event develops between June and August.
item.content:encoded: The World Meteorological Organisation said there is an 80 per cent chance that an El Nino event develops between June and August.

item.title: Kremlin Accuses Ukraine Of Committing "Acts Of Terror" Against Civilians

media:content.media:description: Moscow has said it is stepping up its strikes on military targets in Ukraine.

item.description: Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov made the remarks to reporters when asked about Russia's massive overnight strikes on Ukrainian cities, which killed at least 17 people and wounded 100.
item.content:encoded: Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov made the remarks to reporters when asked about Russia's massive overnight strikes on Ukrainian cities, which killed at least 17 people and wounded 100.

item.title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Iranian Officer Says Renewed War With US 'Inevitable' Amid Trump's Peace Push
item.description: US-Iran war live updates: The remark by Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf came as reports suggested US President Donald Trump has sent back a revised, tougher peace proposal
item.content:encoded: US-Iran war live updates: The remark by Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf came as reports suggested US President Donald Trump has sent back a revised, tougher peace proposal

item.title: Bear Attack Leaves 4 Injured In North Japan

media:content.media:description: In the last fiscal year to March, bear sightings nationwide topped 50,000.

item.description: The bear was first spotted in a car parts factory, prompting an emergency call explaining that "employees had been bitten".
item.content:encoded: The bear was first spotted in a car parts factory, prompting an emergency call explaining that "employees had been bitten".

item.title: CCTV Cameras Under Scanner In J&K, Punjab After ISI Spy Network Bust

media:content.media:description: Law enforcement units have now been ordered to physically verify every CCTV installation

item.description: The weaponisation of CCTV cameras by Pakistan's Inter-Services Intelligence has set off an intensive audit of surveillance systems across Jammu & Kashmir and Punjab after investigators uncovered a spy ring using covert cameras to track troop movement
item.content:encoded: The weaponisation of CCTV cameras by Pakistan's Inter-Services Intelligence has set off an intensive audit of surveillance systems across Jammu & Kashmir and Punjab after investigators uncovered a spy ring using covert cameras to track troop movement

item.title: Video: Man Dresses As Lord Ram In Canada Store, Crowd Chants "Jai Shri Ram"

media:content.media:description: Internet users are defending the individuals by saying that the location is an Indian grocery store

item.description: Canada has multicultural immigration patterns, but this viral clip has opened a larger debate about how immigrants navigate host societies, shifting demographics and the behavioural expectations placed upon them.
item.content:encoded: Canada has multicultural immigration patterns, but this viral clip has opened a larger debate about how immigrants navigate host societies, shifting demographics and the behavioural expectations placed upon them.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | There Is A 'Shadow' Route Keeping Iran Alive, And America Can't Touch It
item.description: If the Strait of Hormuz is Iran's Trump card, then the Caspian Sea in its north is the 'war machine' helping Iran play its cards well.
item.content:encoded: If the Strait of Hormuz is Iran's Trump card, then the Caspian Sea in its north is the 'war machine' helping Iran play its cards well.

item.title: From Enemies To Friends: Russia Signs Military Pact With Taliban

media:content.media:description: Analysts say the agreement is more likely to focus on security cooperation

item.description: Russia has signed a military cooperation agreement with the Taliban, further strengthening ties with Afghanistan's ruling group and reinforcing its position as the only country to officially recognise the Taliban government.
item.content:encoded: Russia has signed a military cooperation agreement with the Taliban, further strengthening ties with Afghanistan's ruling group and reinforcing its position as the only country to officially recognise the Taliban government.

item.title: "Apocalypse": Kyiv Residents Describe Russian Attack That Killed 18

media:content.media:description: Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities awoke on Tuesday to the wail of air raid alerts.

item.description: Civilians are also caught up in the horror, with at least 18 people killed and more than 100 wounded in the latest Russian strikes.
item.content:encoded: Civilians are also caught up in the horror, with at least 18 people killed and more than 100 wounded in the latest Russian strikes.

item.title: Russia Accuses Kyiv Of 'Acts Of Terror' After Attacks On Ukrainian Cities

media:content.media:description: Russia made overnight strikes on Ukrainian cities, which killed at least 17 people

item.description: Moscow has said it is stepping up its strikes on military targets in Ukraine in retaliation for what it said was a devastating Ukrainian drone strike.
item.content:encoded: Moscow has said it is stepping up its strikes on military targets in Ukraine in retaliation for what it said was a devastating Ukrainian drone strike.

item.title: Video: Trash Piles Up At Everest Camp, Tents And Oxygen Bottles Left Behind

media:content.media:description: The video shows various items left behind at the campsite.

item.description: The video has sparked discussion online about the environmental impact of climbing activities on Mount Everest.
item.content:encoded: The video has sparked discussion online about the environmental impact of climbing activities on Mount Everest.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Why Pete Hegseth's Unusual 'Shangri-La' Speech Should Worry India
item.description: Usually, the forum is not the stuff of headlines. This year, though, almost every country seemed uneasy, thanks to Hegseth.
item.content:encoded: Usually, the forum is not the stuff of headlines. This year, though, almost every country seemed uneasy, thanks to Hegseth.

item.title: "Anthropic Thinks It's Creating A God" Say Veteran Investors

media:content.media:description: Anthropic, the creator of Claude, is one of the world's leading AI companies

item.description: Bill Gurley, a veteran investor and part of Uber's early funding round, also compared Anthropic to Frankenstein's monster. He was in conversation with Jason (McCabe) Calacanis, also an early Uber investor, who said Anthropic is suffering from "delusi
item.content:encoded: Bill Gurley, a veteran investor and part of Uber's early funding round, also compared Anthropic to Frankenstein's monster. He was in conversation with Jason (McCabe) Calacanis, also an early Uber investor, who said Anthropic is suffering from "delusi

item.title: 'Raped By 600-700 Men': UK MP Shares 'Pak Grooming Gang' Testimonies

media:content.media:description: Lowe led a private investigation that identified "gang-based child sexual exploitation" in UK

item.description: According to testimonies, gang members used racial comparisons to justify the way they "treated and controlled" women.
item.content:encoded: According to testimonies, gang members used racial comparisons to justify the way they "treated and controlled" women.
